nodes.title: Import the template dependencies
nodes.why: The standalone root component owns its imports, and FormsModule is required for the ngModel bindings used by the todo input and checkbox.
nodes.alternative: Rely on an NgModule or omit FormsModule from the standalone component.
nodes.tradeoff: Keeping imports local makes the exercise self-contained, but every template dependency must be listed explicitly.

nodes.title: Keep todo state local and explicit
nodes.why: A local array plus an id counter is enough for add, toggle, remove, and clear-completed behavior in this component-sized exercise.
nodes.alternative: Introduce a service, store, or DOM-derived state for a small local interaction.
nodes.tradeoff: Local component state is easy to test and read, while larger apps may later move shared todo state elsewhere.

nodes.title: Reject empty todo text before mutation
nodes.why: Trimming first prevents whitespace-only todos from entering the rendered list or persisted state.
nodes.alternative: Push every input value and clean the list later.
nodes.tradeoff: The early return adds a small branch, but it keeps downstream rendering and tests simpler.

nodes.title: Append items with stable identity
nodes.why: The generated id gives Angular @for track todo.id a stable identity for each row as items are added and removed.
nodes.alternative: Track by array index or reuse the text as an identity.
nodes.tradeoff: An id counter is small bookkeeping, but it avoids row identity drift when duplicate text or removals occur.

nodes.title: Toggle the selected todo only
nodes.why: The checkbox interaction should change completion for the row the user touched without rebuilding unrelated items.
nodes.alternative: Infer completion from CSS classes or toggle every item through shared state.
nodes.tradeoff: Directly mutating the item is concise for this local model; immutable updates can be preferred when state is shared.

nodes.title: Remove by stable id
nodes.why: Filtering by id deletes the exact row even when multiple todos have the same text.
nodes.alternative: Remove by visible label or by current array index.
nodes.tradeoff: Id-based removal needs a generated id, but it remains correct after sorting, filtering, or duplicate entries.

nodes.title: Derive the remaining active list
nodes.why: Clear completed should preserve active todos and remove only items already marked done.
nodes.alternative: Reset the entire list or manually splice completed rows in place.
nodes.tradeoff: A filter pass is simple and predictable for this list size, though very large lists might need a different data model.
